name: Weather Widget Overlay UI

intent.description: simple weather-app-style overlay for showing day-of-run weather and temperature context
avoid: decorative background art behind the widgets

presets.label: Simple Card
presets.notes: default preset; blue translucent card with vertical divider, compact left visual block, and location/country slot

presets.label: Forecast Tile
presets.notes: larger square-ish tile for title-card or social clips; configurable horizontal and vertical dividers separate header, temperature, and metric areas

presets.label: Minimal Text
presets.notes: text-first treatment with no heavy container

presets.label: Dashboard Bar
presets.notes: wide weather dashboard bar with taller metric chips for readable labels and values

rules: all icons use the same family and optical weight
rounded filled geometric shapes are preferred
icons must scale cleanly to 20, 32, 48, and 64 px
do not mix emoji, realistic, outline-only, and cartoon treatments

phase2: Open-Meteo historical API by activity timestamp
activity-location fetch by first FIT GPS route point
current-location fetch by CoreLocation device coordinate

inspectorSectionsHiddenForV1.reason: Shared overlay background controls do not affect the Weather Widget SwiftUI preset renderer.
Shared overlay border controls do not affect the Weather Widget SwiftUI preset renderer.
Shared overlay effects controls do not affect the Weather Widget SwiftUI preset renderer.

inspectorControlsImplemented: manual humidity, high/low, wind, feels-like fields for selected slots outside Open-Meteo mode

inspectorControlsHiddenForV1.reason: Card surface color is palette-owned in Weather Widget 1.0; the old field did not reliably affect the preset renderer.
Preset switching is already handled by the compact Styles icon buttons.
Icon size is preset-owned in Weather Widget 1.0.

inspectorControlsHiddenForV1.control: manual API-owned text inputs in Open-Meteo mode
inspectorControlsHiddenForV1.reason: When the widget uses API data, condition, temperature, and metric values come from cached weather payloads.

metricSlots.noneBehavior: selected '-' slots render no metric content
metricSlots.inlineFeelsLikeLabel: Inline metric rows render feelsLike as 'Feels 12°C'; Dashboard Bar keeps 'Feels' as the chip label and '12°C' as the value.

dividers.hiddenBehavior: when disabled, preset divider lines are not rendered; color, width, and opacity values are preserved but hidden from the Inspector

implementationNotes: weather widget should become a dedicated overlay type, not only a numeric temperature preset
preview and export must share the same render layout
API-backed weather must be cached before export
fetch actions write cachedWeather, switch dataSource to Open-Meteo, and update locationText from reverse geocoding
Open-Meteo archive request uses temperature_2m, relative_humidity_2m, apparent_temperature, weather_code, and wind_speed_10m
SwiftUI preview/export uses the shared custom weather icon family
Core Graphics PNG weather rendering is deprecated and no longer a target for parity
